mentions.note: Networking infrastructure; long-time industry leader benefiting from data center buildout alongside Arista and Ciena.
5th largest CIBR holding; freight-train chart with higher highs/lows; reporting Wednesday but likely heads to $100, then $110; strong institutional buying.
CEO Chuck Robbins delivered accelerating sales and earnings growth; 13% rally completely justified. Real business momentum, not bubble hype like Cerebras.

Best-performing Dow stock (66% YTD); positioned as infrastructure winner in AI data-center buildout with owned silicon and optical stack. Lieba/Silicon One and Acacia acquisitions provide supply-chain differentiation competitors lack.
December sell-off looks exhausted; a break above the 50-day average could carry shares toward old highs near $80 and eventually $100.
Stock soaring but company mix unclear; more hardware, less software not what tech investors want—need clarity.
Networking equipment supplier; beneficiary of data center buildout and connectivity needs.
